  "text": "The Grafana permission model is built on a comprehensive three-tier role hierarchy (Viewer/Editor/Admin) defined across multiple packages:\n\n**Core Role Definitions:**\nThe three-tier role types are defined in `pkg/services/org/model.go` as `RoleViewer`, `RoleEditor`, and `RoleAdmin`. These are the base organization-level roles.\n\n**Role-to-Permission Mapping:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` declares fixed roles and registers their assignments to the three built-in roles through the `DeclareFixedRoles()` function. The `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` function creates the basic role definitions with empty permissions initially. \n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` explicitly maps dashboard permissions to the three tiers:\n- `DashboardViewActions` (read-only): assigned to \"Viewer\" role\n- `DashboardEditActions` (read+write+delete): assigned to \"Editor\" role  \n- `DashboardAdminActions` (read+write+delete+permissions): assigned to \"Admin\" role\nThe `registerDashboardRoles()` function registers these role-to-permission mappings.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` provides the same three-tier mapping for folders with `FolderViewActions`, `FolderEditActions`, and `FolderAdminActions`, which are also registered via `registerFolderRoles()`.\n\n**Permission Data Models:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` defines the core data structures:\n- `RoleRegistration`: maps roles to their permissions and built-in role grants\n- `Permission`: represents an action-scope pair\n- `Role` and `RoleDTO`: represent stored roles and their metadata\nThe `BuiltInRolesWithParents()` function enforces role hierarchy where child roles inherit parent permissions.\n\n**Permission Guard Middleware:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` implements the `Middleware()` function that wraps HTTP handlers with permission checks. It calls `authorize()` which evaluates permissions using an Evaluator against the user's permissions.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` defines permission evaluation:\n- `Evaluator` interface: represents permission requirements (action+scopes)\n- `EvalPermission()`: creates evaluators for specific actions\n- `EvalAll()` and `EvalAny()`: compose evaluators with AND/OR logic\n- `MutateScopes()`: resolves scope parameters (e.g., converting dashboard IDs to UIDs)\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/checker.go` implements `Checker()` which creates a closure that quickly checks if a user has specific scopes for an action using wildcard matching.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/authorizer.go` provides `LegacyAccessClient` for authorization compatibility with k8s-style verbs, mapping them to RBAC actions.\n\n**Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance:**\n`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` implements permission inheritance:\n- `GetInheritedScopes()`: retrieves ancestor folder UIDs via `folderSvc.GetParents()`, returning their scopes\n- `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves folder scopes to include parent folder scopes\n- `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves dashboard scopes to include both dashboard and parent folder scopes\n- `resolveDashboardScope()`: combines dashboard and folder scopes for permission evaluation\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go` implements the data layer filtering:\n- `NewAccessControlDashboardPermissionFilter()` creates filters with action sets mapping (e.g., \"folders:view\", \"folders:edit\", \"folders:admin\")\n- `accessControlDashboardPermissionFilter` applies SQL WHERE clauses that check both dashboard and inherited folder permissions\n\n**Low-Level Permission Types:**\n`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` defines the legacy three-tier `PermissionType` enum:\n- `PERMISSION_VIEW`: read access\n- `PERMISSION_EDIT`: write access\n- `PERMISSION_ADMIN`: admin access\nThis is converted to action-based permissions in the access control layer.\n\n**Main Interface:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` defines:\n- `AccessControl` interface: evaluates permissions with `Evaluate()`\n- `Service` interface: manages roles and permissions with `DeclareFixedRoles()` and `GetUserPermissions()`\nThe service integrates all components to provide the complete permission enforcement system."
}

## Summary

I've identified **13 core Go files** and **43 key symbols** that define and enforce Grafana's three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin):

### Key Components Found:

**1. Role Definitions & Mapping (3 files)**
- `pkg/services/org/model.go` - Core role types (RoleViewer, RoleEditor, RoleAdmin)
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` - Fixed role declarations with permission assignments
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` - Role and Permission data structures

**2. Three-Tier Hierarchy (2 files)**
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` - Maps DashboardViewActions/EditActions/AdminActions to the three roles
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` - Maps FolderViewActions/EditActions/AdminActions to the three roles

**3. Permission Guard Middleware (4 files)**
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` - HTTP middleware that enforces permission checks
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` - Permission evaluation logic (EvalPermission, EvalAll, EvalAny)
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/checker.go` - Checker function for scope-based permission validation
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/authorizer.go` - Resource authorizer with legacy k8s verb support

**4. Folder/Dashboard Inheritance (2 files)**
- `pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` - GetInheritedScopes() resolves parent folder permissions
- `pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go` - SQL filters that apply inherited folder permissions

**5. Supporting Files (2 files)**
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` - Main AccessControl interface and Service
- `pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` - Legacy PERMISSION_VIEW/EDIT/ADMIN constants

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with complete file paths, symbols, execution chains, and a detailed narrative explaining how these components work together.

   371→func ConcatPermissions(permissions ...[]Permission) []Permission {
   372→	if permissions == nil {
   373→		return nil
   374→	}
   375→	perms := make([]Permission, 0)
   376→	for _, p := range permissions {
   377→		pCopy := make([]Permission, 0, len(p))
   378→		copy(pCopy, p)
   379→		perms = append(perms, p...)
   380→	}
   381→	return perms
   382→}

   384→// PrefixedRoleUID generates a uid from name with the same prefix.
   385→// Generated uid is 28 bytes + length of prefix: <prefix>_base64(sha1(roleName))
   386→func PrefixedRoleUID(roleName string) string {
   387→	prefix := strings.Split(roleName, ":")[0] + "_"
   388→
   389→	// #nosec G505 Used only for generating a 160 bit hash, it's not used for security purposes
   390→	hasher := sha1.New()
   391→	hasher.Write([]byte(roleName))
   392→
   393→	return fmt.Sprintf("%s%s", prefix, base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(hasher.Sum(nil)))
   394→}

   398→	if !strings.HasPrefix(role.Name, FixedRolePrefix) {

   404→// ValidateBuiltInRoles errors when a built-in role does not match expected pattern
   405→func ValidateBuiltInRoles(builtInRoles []string) error {
   406→	for _, br := range builtInRoles {
   407→		if org.RoleType(br) == org.RoleNone {
   408→			return ErrNoneRoleAssignment
   409→		}
   410→		if !org.RoleType(br).IsValid() && br != RoleGrafanaAdmin {
   411→			return ErrInvalidBuiltinRole.Build(ErrInvalidBuiltinRoleData(br))
   412→		}
   413→	}
   414→	return nil
   415→}

   417→type RegistrationList struct {
   418→	mx            sync.RWMutex
   419→	registrations []RoleRegistration
   420→}
   421→
   422→func (m *RegistrationList) Append(regs ...RoleRegistration) {
   423→	m.mx.Lock()
   424→	defer m.mx.Unlock()
   425→	m.registrations = append(m.registrations, regs...)
   426→}
   427→
   428→func (m *RegistrationList) Range(f func(registration RoleRegistration) bool) {
   429→	m.mx.RLock()
   430→	defer m.mx.RUnlock()
   431→	for _, registration := range m.registrations {
   432→		if ok := f(registration); !ok {
   433→			return
   434→		}
   435→	}
   436→}
   437→
   438→func (m *RegistrationList) Slice() []RoleRegistration {
   439→	m.mx.RLock()
   440→	defer m.mx.RUnlock()
   441→	out := make([]RoleRegistration, len(m.registrations))
   442→	copy(out, m.registrations)
   443→	return out
   444→}

   254→func (p *ResourcePermission) Contains(targetActions []string) bool {
   255→	if len(p.Actions) < len(targetActions) {
   256→		return false
   257→	}
   258→
   259→	var contain = func(arr []string, s string) bool {
   260→		for _, item := range arr {
   261→			if item == s {
   262→				return true
   263→			}
   264→		}
   265→		return false
   266→	}
   267→
   268→	for _, a := range targetActions {
   269→		if !contain(p.Actions, a) {
   270→			return false
   271→		}
   272→	}
   273→
   274→	return true
   275→}

   286→	ExternalServiceID string
   287→	ServiceAccountID  int64
   288→	Permissions       []Permission

   290→
   291→func (cmd *SaveExternalServiceRoleCommand) Validate() error {
   292→	if cmd.ExternalServiceID == "" {
   293→		return errors.New("external service id not specified")
   294→	}
   295→
   296→	// slugify the external service id ID for the role to have correct name and uid
   297→	cmd.ExternalServiceID = slugify.Slugify(cmd.ExternalServiceID)
   298→
   299→	// Check and deduplicate permissions
   300→	if len(cmd.Permissions) == 0 {
   301→		return errors.New("no permissions provided")
   302→	}
   303→	dedupMap := map[Permission]bool{}
   304→	dedup := make([]Permission, 0, len(cmd.Permissions))
   305→	for i := range cmd.Permissions {
   306→		if len(cmd.Permissions[i].Action) == 0 {
   307→			return fmt.Errorf("external service %v requests a permission with no Action", cmd.ExternalServiceID)
   308→		}
   309→		if dedupMap[cmd.Permissions[i]] {
   310→			continue
   311→		}
   312→		dedupMap[cmd.Permissions[i]] = true
   313→		cmd.Permissions[i].Kind, cmd.Permissions[i].Attribute, cmd.Permissions[i].Identifier = SplitScope(cmd.Permissions[i].Scope)
   314→		dedup = append(dedup, cmd.Permissions[i])
   315→	}
   316→	cmd.Permissions = dedup
   317→
   318→	if cmd.ServiceAccountID <= 0 {
   319→		return fmt.Errorf("invalid service account id %d", cmd.ServiceAccountID)
   320→	}
   321→
   322→	return nil
   323→}

    30→func Middleware(ac AccessControl) func(Evaluator) web.Handler {
    31→	return func(evaluator Evaluator) web.Handler {
    32→		return func(c *contextmodel.ReqContext) {
    33→			ctx, span := tracer.Start(c.Req.Context(), "accesscontrol.Middleware")
    34→			defer span.End()
    35→			c.Req = c.Req.WithContext(ctx)
    36→
    37→			if c.AllowAnonymous {
    38→				forceLogin, _ := strconv.ParseBool(c.Req.URL.Query().Get("forceLogin")) // ignoring error, assuming false for non-true values is ok.
    39→				orgID, err := strconv.ParseInt(c.Req.URL.Query().Get("orgId"), 10, 64)
    40→				if err == nil && orgID > 0 && orgID != c.GetOrgID() {
    41→					forceLogin = true
    42→				}
    43→
    44→				if !c.IsSignedIn && forceLogin {
    45→					unauthorized(c)
    46→					return
    47→				}
    48→			}
    49→
    50→			if c.LookupTokenErr != nil {
    51→				var revokedErr *usertoken.TokenRevokedError
    52→				if errors.As(c.LookupTokenErr, &revokedErr) {
    53→					tokenRevoked(c, revokedErr)
    54→					return
    55→				}
    56→
    57→				unauthorized(c)
    58→				return
    59→			}
    60→
    61→			authorize(c, ac, c.SignedInUser, evaluator)
    62→		}
    63→	}
    64→}

    66→func authorize(c *contextmodel.ReqContext, ac AccessControl, user identity.Requester, evaluator Evaluator) {
    67→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(c.Req.Context(), "accesscontrol.authorize")
    68→	defer span.End()
    69→	c.Req = c.Req.WithContext(ctx)
    70→
    71→	injected, err := evaluator.MutateScopes(ctx, scopeInjector(scopeParams{
    72→		OrgID:     user.GetOrgID(),
    73→		URLParams: web.Params(c.Req),
    74→	}))
    75→	if err != nil {
    76→		c.JsonApiErr(http.StatusInternalServerError, "Internal server error", err)
    77→		return
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	hasAccess, err := ac.Evaluate(ctx, user, injected)
    81→	if !hasAccess || err != nil {
    82→		deny(c, injected, err)
    83→		return
    84→	}
    85→}

    87→func deny(c *contextmodel.ReqContext, evaluator Evaluator, err error) {
    88→	id := newID()

  "text": "The Grafana permission model is built on a comprehensive three-tier role hierarchy (Viewer/Editor/Admin) defined across multiple packages:\n\n**Core Role Definitions:**\nThe three-tier role types are defined in `pkg/services/org/model.go` as `RoleViewer`, `RoleEditor`, and `RoleAdmin`. These are the base organization-level roles.\n\n**Role-to-Permission Mapping:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` declares fixed roles and registers their assignments to the three built-in roles through the `DeclareFixedRoles()` function. The `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` function creates the basic role definitions with empty permissions initially. \n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` explicitly maps dashboard permissions to the three tiers:\n- `DashboardViewActions` (read-only): assigned to \"Viewer\" role\n- `DashboardEditActions` (read+write+delete): assigned to \"Editor\" role  \n- `DashboardAdminActions` (read+write+delete+permissions): assigned to \"Admin\" role\nThe `registerDashboardRoles()` function registers these role-to-permission mappings.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` provides the same three-tier mapping for folders with `FolderViewActions`, `FolderEditActions`, and `FolderAdminActions`, which are also registered via `registerFolderRoles()`.\n\n**Permission Data Models:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` defines the core data structures:\n- `RoleRegistration`: maps roles to their permissions and built-in role grants\n- `Permission`: represents an action-scope pair\n- `Role` and `RoleDTO`: represent stored roles and their metadata\nThe `BuiltInRolesWithParents()` function enforces role hierarchy where child roles inherit parent permissions.\n\n**Permission Guard Middleware:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` implements the `Middleware()` function that wraps HTTP handlers with permission checks. interface: represents permission requirements (action+scopes)\n- `EvalPermission()`: creates evaluators for specific actions\n- `EvalAll()` and `EvalAny()`: compose evaluators with AND/OR logic\n- `MutateScopes()`: resolves scope parameters (e.g., converting dashboard IDs to UIDs)\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/checker.go` implements `Checker()` which creates a closure that quickly checks if a user has specific scopes for an action using wildcard matching.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/authorizer.go` provides `LegacyAccessClient` for authorization compatibility with k8s-style verbs, mapping them to RBAC actions.\n\n**Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance:**\n`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` implements permission inheritance:\n- `GetInheritedScopes()`: retrieves ancestor folder UIDs via `folderSvc.GetParents()`, returning their scopes\n- `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves folder scopes to include parent folder scopes\n- `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves dashboard scopes to include both dashboard and parent folder scopes\n- `resolveDashboardScope()`: combines dashboard and folder scopes for permission evaluation\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go` implements the data layer filtering:\n- `NewAccessControlDashboardPermissionFilter()` creates filters with action sets mapping (e.g., \"folders:view\", \"folders:edit\", \"folders:admin\")\n- `accessControlDashboardPermissionFilter` applies SQL WHERE clauses that check both dashboard and inherited folder permissions\n\n**Low-Level Permission Types:**\n`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` defines the legacy three-tier `PermissionType` enum:\n- `PERMISSION_VIEW`: read access\n- `PERMISSION_EDIT`: write access\n- `PERMISSION_ADMIN`: admin access\nThis is converted to action-based permissions in the access control layer.\n\n**Main Interface:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` defines:\n- `AccessControl` interface: evaluates permissions with `Evaluate()`\n- `Service` interface: manages roles and permissions with `DeclareFixedRoles()` and `GetUserPermissions()`\nThe service integrates all components to provide the complete permission enforcement system."
}

    66→  "text": "The Grafana permission model is built on a comprehensive three-tier role hierarchy (Viewer/Editor/Admin) defined across multiple packages:

**Core Role Definitions:**
The three-tier role types are defined in `pkg/services/org/model.go` as `RoleViewer`, `RoleEditor`, and `RoleAdmin`. These are the base organization-level roles.

**Role-to-Permission Mapping:**
`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` declares fixed roles and registers their assignments to the three built-in roles through the `DeclareFixedRoles()` function. The `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` function creates the basic role definitions with empty permissions initially. 

`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` explicitly maps dashboard permissions to the three tiers:
- `DashboardViewActions` (read-only): assigned to "Viewer" role
- `DashboardEditActions` (read+write+delete): assigned to "Editor" role  
- `DashboardAdminActions` (read+write+delete+permissions): assigned to "Admin" role
The `registerDashboardRoles()` function registers these role-to-permission mappings.

`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` provides the same three-tier mapping for folders with `FolderViewActions`, `FolderEditActions`, and `FolderAdminActions`, which are also registered via `registerFolderRoles()`.

**Permission Data Models:**
`pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` defines the core data structures:
- `RoleRegistration`: maps roles to their permissions and built-in role grants
- `Permission`: represents an action-scope pair
- `Role` and `RoleDTO`: represent stored roles and their metadata
The `BuiltInRolesWithParents()` function enforces role hierarchy where child roles inherit parent permissions.

**Permission Guard Middleware:**
`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` implements the `Middleware()` function that wraps HTTP handlers with permission checks. It calls `authorize()` which evaluates permissions using an Evaluator against the user's permissions.

`pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` defines permission evaluation:
- `Evaluator` interface: represents permission requirements (action+scopes)
- `EvalPermission()`: creates evaluators for specific actions
- `EvalAll()` and `EvalAny()`: compose evaluators with AND/OR logic
- `MutateScopes()`: resolves scope parameters (e.g., converting dashboard IDs to UIDs)

`pkg/services/accesscontrol/checker.go` implements `Checker()` which creates a closure that quickly checks if a user has specific scopes for an action using wildcard matching.

`pkg/services/accesscontrol/authorizer.go` provides `LegacyAccessClient` for authorization compatibility with k8s-style verbs, mapping them to RBAC actions.

**Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance:**
`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` implements permission inheritance:
- `GetInheritedScopes()`: retrieves ancestor folder UIDs via `folderSvc.GetParents()`, returning their scopes
- `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves folder scopes to include parent folder scopes
- `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves dashboard scopes to include both dashboard and parent folder scopes
- `resolveDashboardScope()`: combines dashboard and folder scopes for permission evaluation

`pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go` implements the data layer filtering:
- `NewAccessControlDashboardPermissionFilter()` creates filters with action sets mapping (e.g., "folders:view", "folders:edit", "folders:admin")
- `accessControlDashboardPermissionFilter` applies SQL WHERE clauses that check both dashboard and inherited folder permissions

**Low-Level Permission Types:**
`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` defines the legacy three-tier `PermissionType` enum:
- `PERMISSION_VIEW`: read access
- `PERMISSION_EDIT`: write access
- `PERMISSION_ADMIN`: admin access
This is converted to action-based permissions in the access control layer.

**Main Interface:**
`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` defines:
- `AccessControl` interface: evaluates permissions with `Evaluate()`
- `Service` interface: manages roles and permissions with `DeclareFixedRoles()` and `GetUserPermissions()`
The service integrates all components to provide the complete permission enforcement system."

The `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` function creates the basic role definitions with empty permissions initially. \n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` explicitly maps dashboard permissions to the three tiers:\n- `DashboardViewActions` (read-only): assigned to \"Viewer\" role\n- `DashboardEditActions` (read+write+delete): assigned to \"Editor\" role  \n- `DashboardAdminActions` (read+write+delete+permissions): assigned to \"Admin\" role\nThe `registerDashboardRoles()` function registers these role-to-permission mappings.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` provides the same three-tier mapping for folders with `FolderViewActions`, `FolderEditActions`, and `FolderAdminActions`, which are also registered via `registerFolderRoles()`.\n\n**Permission Data Models:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` defines the core data structures:\n- `RoleRegistration`: maps roles to their permissions and built-in role grants\n- `Permission`: represents an action-scope pair\n- `Role` and `RoleDTO`: represent stored roles and their metadata\nThe `BuiltInRolesWithParents()` function enforces role hierarchy where child roles inherit parent permissions.\n\n**Permission Guard Middleware:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` implements the `Middleware()` function that wraps HTTP handlers with permission checks. It calls `authorize()` which evaluates permissions using an Evaluator against the user's permissions.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` defines permission evaluation:\n- `Evaluator` interface: represents permission requirements (action+scopes)\n- `EvalPermission()`: creates evaluators for specific actions\n- `EvalAll()` and `EvalAny()`: compose evaluators with AND/OR logic\n- `MutateScopes()`: resolves scope parameters (e.g., converting dashboard IDs to UIDs)\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/checker.go` implements `Checker()` which creates a closure that quickly checks if a user has specific scopes for an action using wildcard matching.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/authorizer.go` provides `LegacyAccessClient` for authorization compatibility with k8s-style verbs, mapping them to RBAC actions.\n\n**Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance:**\n`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` implements permission inheritance:\n- `GetInheritedScopes()`: retrieves ancestor folder UIDs via `folderSvc.GetParents()`, returning their scopes\n- `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves folder scopes to include parent folder scopes\n- `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves dashboard scopes to include both dashboard and parent folder scopes\n- `resolveDashboardScope()`: combines dashboard and folder scopes for permission evaluation\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go` implements the data layer filtering:\n- `NewAccessControlDashboardPermissionFilter()` creates filters with action sets mapping (e.g., \"folders:view\", \"folders:edit\", \"folders:admin\")\n- `accessControlDashboardPermissionFilter` applies SQL WHERE clauses that check both dashboard and inherited folder permissions\n\n**Low-Level Permission Types:**\n`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` defines the legacy three-tier `PermissionType` enum:\n- `PERMISSION_VIEW`: read access\n- `PERMISSION_EDIT`: write access\n- `PERMISSION_ADMIN`: admin access\nThis is converted to action-based permissions in the access control layer.\n\n**Main Interface:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` defines:\n- `AccessControl` interface: evaluates permissions with `Evaluate()`\n- `Service` interface: manages roles and permissions with `DeclareFixedRoles()` and `GetUserPermissions()`\nThe service integrates all components to provide the complete permission enforcement system."
